name: Protagonists & Perspective
description: Who carries the story and how closely the reader experiences their point of view.

name: Relationships & Dynamics
description: Romantic, familial, platonic, professional, and adversarial bonds.

name: Roles & Callings
description: Occupations, vocations, social functions, and identities that generate story pressure.

name: Bodies & Lived Experience
description: Embodiment, access, health, age, identity, and life circumstances.

name: Places & Journeys
description: Locations, landscapes, built environments, and ways of moving through them.

name: Societies & Institutions
description: Governments, economies, communities, hierarchies, and collective systems.

name: Magic & Wonder
description: Sources, costs, expressions, institutions, and consequences of the impossible.

name: Science & Futures
description: Scientific ideas, future technologies, altered humanity, and life beyond Earth.

name: Secrets & Schemes
description: Conspiracies, hidden histories, deceptions, investigations, and intricate plans.

name: Peril & Survival
description: Danger, isolation, rescue, disaster, pursuit, and pressure-driven stories.

name: Change & Consequence
description: Character trajectories, reversals, moral choices, rises, falls, and second chances.

name: Story Shapes
description: Chronology, viewpoint architecture, framing devices, and structural experiments.

name: Mood & Atmosphere
description: Emotional weather, sensory atmosphere, and the story's dominant feeling.

name: Pace & Scale
description: Narrative momentum, scope, intensity, and the size of the story's canvas.

name: Style & Sensibility
description: Prose texture, comic mode, aesthetic posture, and storytelling sensibility.
